figure_details.visual_quality_notes: All four figures render cleanly with serif fonts, consistent color palette, and clear labels. fig_firing_rates (bar chart, 2 panels) has correct values (5.2/20, 5.5/21, 7.8/26, 15/42 Hz) with numeric annotations on bars. fig_model_vs_experiment correctly shows 55/50/58 Hz and 18/20 ms. fig_network_schematic is a well-labeled architecture diagram grounded in NE=4000, NI=1000, p=0.2, synaptic types (AMPA/NMDA/GABA) and external input rate vX=5 Hz. fig_state_diagram is appropriately labeled as a schematic illustration with the boundary shape disclaimer, showing the two primary networks (steady circle, critical star). All figures are referenced in the text via \ref labels (fig:schematic, fig:state_diagram, fig:firing_rates, fig:model_vs_experiment).

brief_notes: Strong paper that faithfully reproduces the source findings. All key numerical claims are verified against the extraction: N=5000 (NE=4000, NI=1000), p=0.2, firing rates 5.2/20, 5.5/21, 7.8/26, 15/42 Hz, ~50 Hz simulated oscillation, 58 Hz theoretical onset, ~55 Hz experimental, +/-18 ms (experimental) and +/-20 ms (model) correlation lags, 978/368 and 1246/434 neuron pair counts, PCP dose 0.25-0.30 mg/kg IM, DPX task timing (1000/1000/500 ms), AMPA/NMDA/GABA time constants, Jahr-Stevens Mg block parameters (beta=0.062, gamma=3.57, [Mg]=1), neuron membrane params (Cm, gm, tau_rp). Writing is well-organized with clear Introduction, Background, Methods (with three parameter tables), Results, and Discussion sections. Citations are appropriate and densely placed (Brunel, van Vreeswijk, Zick, Jahr-Stevens, etc.). Figures are publication-quality; the state diagram is honestly labeled as schematic since exact boundary curve data is not in the extraction. One minor note: the abstract mentions 'critical state 5.5/21 at baseline' which matches; no hallucinated numbers detected. Model-experiment comparison table is a nice addition. No placeholder figures hiding missing data. Overall this is a high-quality v3_orch_plot output.
